children.text: it is in the highest national security interests of the United States to develop covered energy sources;
the State of Israel is a steadfast ally of the United States;

children.label: A
children.text: the United States-Israel Binational Science Foundation; and

children.label: B
children.text: the United States-Israel Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation;

children.label: A
children.text: many scientific, technological, and commercial breakthroughs in the fields of life sciences, medicine, bioengineering, agriculture, biotechnology, communications, and others; and

children.label: B
children.text: significant contributions to the development of renewable energy and energy efficiency through the established programs of the United States-Israel Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation and the United States-Israel Binational Science Foundation;

on , the Secretary of Energy (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”) and the Israeli Minister of Energy and Infrastructure signed an agreement to establish a framework for collaboration between the United States and Israel in energy research and development activities;February 1, 1996
Israeli scientists and engineers are at the forefront of research and development in the field of covered energy sources;
enhanced cooperation between the United States and Israel for the purpose of research and development of covered energy sources would be in the national interests of both countries;
United States-Israel energy cooperation and the development of natural resources by Israel are in the strategic interest of the United States;
Israel is a strategic partner of the United States in water technology;
the United States can play a role in assisting Israel with regional safety and security issues;

the National Science Foundation of the United States, to the extent consistent with the National Science Foundation’s mission, should collaborate with the Israel Science Foundation and the United States-Israel Binational Science Foundation;

children.label: A
children.text: energy innovation technology and engineering;

children.label: B
children.text: water science;

children.label: C
children.text: technology transfer; and

children.label: D
children.text: analysis of emerging geopolitical implications, crises and threats from foreign natural resource and energy acquisitions, and the development of domestic resources as a response;
the United States supports the goals of the Alternative Fuels Administration of Israel with respect to expanding the use of alternative fuels;

children.label: A
children.text: identifying policy priorities associated with the development of natural resources of Israel;

children.label: B
children.text: discussing and sharing best practices to secure cyber energy infrastructure and other energy security matters;

children.label: C
children.text: leveraging natural gas to positively impact regional stability;

children.label: D
children.text: issues relating to the energy-water nexus, including improving energy efficiency and the overall performance of water technologies through research and development in water desalination, wastewater treatment and reclamation, water treatment in gas and oil production processes, and other water treatment refiners;

children.label: E
children.text: technical and environmental management of deep-water exploration and production;

children.label: F
children.text: emergency response and coastal protection and restoration;

children.label: G
children.text: academic outreach and engagement;

children.label: H
children.text: private sector and business development engagement;

children.label: I
children.text: regulatory consultations;

children.label: J
children.text: leveraging alternative transportation fuels and technologies; and

children.label: K
children.text: any other areas determined appropriate by the United States and Israel;

children.label: A
children.text: acknowledges the achievements and importance of the Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation and the United States-Israel Binational Science Foundation; and

children.label: B
children.text: supports continued multiyear funding to ensure the continuity of the programs of the foundations specified in subparagraph (A); and

the United States and Israel have a shared interest in addressing immediate, near-term, and long-term energy, energy poverty, energy independence, and environmental challenges facing the United States and Israel, respectively.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: In implementing the agreement entitled the “Agreement between the Department of Energy of the United States of America and the Ministry of Energy and Infrastructure of Israel Concerning Energy Cooperation”, dated , the Secretary shall establish a grant program in accordance with the requirements of sections 16352 and 16353 of this title to support research, development, and commercialization of covered energy.February 1, 1996

children.label: A
children.text: solar energy;

children.label: B
children.text: biomass energy;

children.label: C
children.text: energy efficiency;

children.label: D
children.text: wind energy;

children.label: E
children.text: geothermal energy;

children.label: F
children.text: wave and tidal energy;

children.label: G
children.text: advanced battery technology;

children.label: H
children.text: natural gas energy, including conventional and unconventional natural gas technologies and other associated technologies, and natural gas projects conducted by or in conjunction with the United States-Israel Binational Science Foundation and the United States-Israel Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation; and

children.label: I
children.text: improvement of energy efficiency and the overall performance of water technologies through research and development in water desalination, wastewater treatment and reclamation, and other water treatment refiners.

children.label: A
children.text: addresses a requirement in the area of improved covered energy sources, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: I
children.text: a for-profit business entity, academic institution, National Laboratory (as defined in ), or nonprofit entity in the United States; andsection 15801 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: a for-profit business entity, academic institution, or nonprofit entity in Israel; or

children.label: I
children.text: the Federal Government; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Government of Israel.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 4 Applications
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this subsection, an applicant shall submit to the Secretary an application for the grant in accordance with procedures established by the Secretary, in consultation with the advisory board established under paragraph (5).

children.label: i
children.text: to monitor the method by which grants are awarded under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide to the Secretary periodic performance reviews of actions taken to carry out this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: 1 shall be a representative of the Federal Government;

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 shall be selected from a list of nominees provided by the United States-Israel Binational Science Foundation; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 1 shall be selected from a list of nominees provided by the United States-Israel Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation.

children.label: A
children.text: without further appropriation; and

children.label: B
children.text: without fiscal year limitation.

children.label: A
children.text: a description of the method by which the recipient used the grant funds; and

children.label: B
children.text: an evaluation of the level of success of each project funded by the grant.

children.heading: Classification
children.label: 8 Classification
children.text: Grants shall be awarded under this subsection only for projects that are considered to be unclassified by both the United States and Israel.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, subject to the availability of appropriations, may enter into cooperative agreements supporting and enhancing dialogue and planning involving international partnerships between the Department, including National Laboratories of the Department, and the Government of Israel and its ministries, offices, and institutions.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: 2 Federal share
children.text: The Secretary may not pay more than 50 percent of Federal share of the costs of implementing cooperative agreements entered into pursuant to paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: actions taken to implement such agreements; and

children.label: B
children.text: any projects undertaken pursuant to such agreements.

children.heading: United States-Israel Energy Center
children.label: d United States-Israel Energy Center
children.text: The Secretary may establish a joint United States-Israel Energy Center in the United States leveraging the experience, knowledge, and expertise of institutions of higher education and entities in the private sector, among others, in offshore energy development to further dialogue and collaboration to develop more robust academic cooperation in energy innovation technology and engineering, water science, technology transfer, and analysis of emerging geopolitical implications, crises and threats from foreign natural resource and energy acquisitions, and the development of domestic resources as a response.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: e Termination
children.text: The grant program and the advisory committee established under this section terminate on .September 30, 2024
